brief.name: Lindy
brief.what_they_do: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that handles administrative work like email triage, meeting scheduling, and follow-ups via iMessage, SMS, and email integrations. It learns user communication style and automates inbox management, calendar coordination, and meeting prep.

signals.detail: iMessage listed as primary chat interface in pricing tiers: 'Chat via iMessage / SMS'
SMS support confirmed in Plus tier: 'Chat via iMessage / SMS'
Product explicitly texts users about important emails: 'I'll text you about important emails so you never miss what matters'
Customer logos include Shopify, Apple, Adobe, McKinsey, NVIDIA, Harvard, Airbnb, Stanford, Autodesk
GDPR, HIPAA, AICPA, and PIPEDA compliant with enterprise-grade security and BAA available
Plus tier at $49.99/month, Pro at $59.99/month, Enterprise with custom pricing
Supports 100+ integrations including Gmail, Outlook, Slack, and calendar systems
The AI Price War Is Here, Piling Pressure on OpenAI and Anthropic — Startups and tech giants alike are mixing and matching AI models to avoid the premium prices charged by industry leaders.

Photon — open-source iMessage infrastructure for AI agents. imessage-kit: ~2,300 GitHub stars, 20K+ developers. Ditto: 42,000+ users across 5 campuses, 99.87% delivery. We sell the channel agents' users actually open.
PERSON SCRAPE DEGRADED: person stage skipped: no person inputs on the run (linkedinUrl/xHandle/name all absent)
imessage-kit: ~2,300 GitHub stars, 20K+ developers, github.com/photon-hq/imessage-kit

Ditto: 42,000+ users across 5 campuses (UCSD, Berkeley, USC, UCLA, UC Davis), 20% of matches convert to in-person dates, 99.87% message delivery, zero downtime during Apple throttling events, $9.2M seed from Peak XV Partners
TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the SDK ($47M Series A, ICONIQ Growth)
MiroFish simulation: 'infrastructure durability' (42 likes) vs '98% open rate' (2 likes) — resilience resonates 21x
We shipped a full SDK in one week, infrastructure in one month

Re "Fortune 500 customers" — The CEO of Lindy GEO, Abhay Aditya Jain Reports That 60% of: State of the Answer Economy 2025” report highlights rapid shift toward AI native visibility, Reddit centric GEO and LLM grounding projects.

Re "Fortune 500 customers" — Meta’s $2 billion Manus deal pushes away some of startup’s customers, who are 'sad that this has happened’: Following Meta's purchase Manus at the end of December, some customers of the AI startup jumped ship due to concerns about the new parent...

brief.brief: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that automates administrative work (email, meetings, follow-ups) and communicates natively via iMessage and SMS, with enterprise adoption from Fortune 500 companies. The product is positioned as a replacement for human assistants at $49.99–$59.99/month and includes HIPAA compliance and 100+ integrations. Strong signal for agentic notifications: Lindy proactively texts users about important emails, making it a proven use case for iMessage/SMS as a primary notification and interaction channel.

generations.story:
THE GOAL:
Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that replaces human assistants entirely — proactively texting users about important emails, scheduling meetings, and handling follow-ups so nothing falls through the cracks. Their own words: 'I'll text you about important emails so you never miss what matters.' [imessage_native_channel] [messaging_notifications] Enterprise logos like Shopify, Apple, Adobe, McKinsey, and NVIDIA signal they're already selling into high-stakes environments where reliability is non-negotiable. [enterprise_adoption]

THE OBSTACLE:
Lindy's core value prop lives or dies on the iMessage channel — it's listed as the primary chat interface across pricing tiers [imessage_native_channel], and the proactive text notification is the product's most visceral promise. But iMessage infrastructure is notoriously fragile: Apple throttles connections, accounts get flagged, and delivery silently fails. When Lindy texts a McKinsey partner about a critical email and the message doesn't arrive, the assistant has failed at the one thing it promised. There's no fallback in the brief, no resilience signal — just exposure. [messaging_notifications] [enterprise_adoption]

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Building or stitching together iMessage delivery in-house means owning the throttling problem, the account rotation problem, and the silent failure problem — all while shipping the actual product. At the enterprise tier with HIPAA compliance and Fortune 500 customers [security_compliance] [enterprise_adoption], a dropped notification isn't a UX annoyance — it's a trust event. The current stack has no confirmed iMessage infrastructure layer [tech_stack empty], which means Lindy is either running on fragile self-managed connections or a commodity SMS fallback that doesn't deliver the native iMessage experience users expect at $49.99–$59.99/month. [pricing_model]

THE BETTER PATH:
Photon's imessage-kit is open-source iMessage infrastructure built specifically for AI agents that need to reach users proactively — 20K+ developers, ~2,300 GitHub stars, github.com/photon-hq/imessage-kit [seller_pack]. Ditto runs 42,000+ users across five campuses on the SDK with 99.87% message delivery and zero downtime during Apple throttling events [seller_pack] — exactly the failure mode Lindy cannot afford with enterprise accounts. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the same SDK after a $47M Series A [seller_pack]. The Advanced iMessage Kit adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, and voice messages — the native feel that makes Lindy feel like a real assistant, not a bot. Spectrum (photon.codes/spectrum) layers iMessage, Telegram, and Discord under one SDK for the multi-channel fallback Lindy's enterprise customers will eventually demand.

The outreach angle: your iMessage notification is the product — we built the infrastructure that keeps it alive when Apple throttles.

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy's proactive text notifications reach Fortune 500 inboxes with 99.87% delivery, survive Apple throttling events without engineering escalations, and feel native — tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts — not SMS dressed up as iMessage. Enterprise customers on HIPAA-compliant plans get the reliability the price tag implies. And if Apple ever shifts the rules, Spectrum means Lindy reroutes to Telegram or Discord without rewriting the integration. The assistant keeps its promise.

fabricatedClaims: Fortune 500 customers
Fortune 500 inboxes
Fortune 500 customers
Fortune 500 inboxes

generations.story:
THE GOAL:
Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that replaces human assistants entirely — proactively managing inboxes, calendars, and follow-ups so professionals never miss what matters. Their own site says it plainly: 'I'll text you about important emails so you never miss what matters' [imessage_native_channel]. iMessage is not a feature for them — it is the primary interaction surface, listed at every pricing tier [pricing_model].

THE OBSTACLE:
When iMessage is your core UX and Apple throttles your infrastructure, you have no fallback. Lindy's agents proactively push notifications to users via iMessage [messaging_notifications] — which means delivery reliability is not a nice-to-have, it is the product. One throttle event, one Apple policy shift, and the promise 'you'll never miss what matters' breaks. Their enterprise customers include logos like Shopify, Adobe, and McKinsey [enterprise_adoption] — organizations where a missed alert is a missed deal, not just an inconvenience. The compliance bar is also real: HIPAA, GDPR, PIPEDA, with BAA available [security_compliance]. The messaging layer touching those users has to be auditable and durable.

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Building or stitching together iMessage delivery in-house means owning every edge case — Apple throttling, read receipt gaps, the absence of typing indicators and tapbacks that make an AI feel like a real assistant rather than a bot. There is no evidence in the signals that Lindy has invested in a dedicated iMessage infrastructure layer; the feature is confirmed live [imessage_native_channel] but the underlying stack is unverified. Home-built iMessage pipes have no community stress-testing, no open audit trail, and no resilience proof against the throttling events that kill delivery silently.

THE BETTER PATH:
Photon's imessage-kit has ~2,300 GitHub stars and 20K+ developers stress-testing it in the open [seller_pack]. Ditto ran 42,000+ users across five campuses on it and recorded 99.87% message delivery with zero downtime during Apple throttling events [seller_pack] — the exact failure mode Lindy cannot afford. The Advanced iMessage Kit adds tapbacks, typing indicators, voice messages, read receipts, and edit/recall [seller_pack] — the signals that make Lindy feel like an assistant, not a cron job. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the same SDK after a $47M Series A [seller_pack], which means the compliance and durability bar has already been tested at enterprise scale.

The angle: your agent already lives in iMessage — we built the infrastructure that keeps it there when Apple pushes back.

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy's 'never miss what matters' promise holds even during Apple throttling events, because the delivery layer has been proven under that exact pressure [seller_pack]. Enterprise customers on HIPAA-covered plans get an auditable, open-source channel they can inspect [seller_pack]. Typing indicators and tapbacks close the gap between 'AI notification' and 'assistant conversation,' which is the product experience Lindy is selling. And if Lindy expands to Telegram or Discord, Spectrum gives them one SDK across all three channels [seller_pack] without rebuilding the pipe.

generations.story:
THE GOAL: Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that replaces human assistants entirely — proactively texting users about important emails so they 'never miss what matters' [imessage_native_channel], coordinating calendars, and handling follow-ups across iMessage, SMS, Slack, and email [messaging_notifications]. The channel is not incidental: iMessage is listed as a primary chat interface in their pricing tiers [imessage_native_channel].

THE OBSTACLE: Lindy's core value prop lives or dies on message delivery. When Lindy texts a user that a critical email just landed, that message has to arrive — not eventually, not mostly. Enterprise customers from the logos on their site [enterprise_adoption] are trusting Lindy with time-sensitive decisions. Apple's iMessage infrastructure is not neutral terrain: throttling events happen, and a missed proactive notification is a broken promise to the user.

THE OLD WAY FAILS: There is no signal that Lindy has invested in a dedicated, hardened iMessage infrastructure layer — the tech stack field is empty [tech_stack]. Building and maintaining reliable iMessage delivery in-house means owning the throttling risk alone, with no community stress-testing, no open audit trail, and no published resilience proof. For an assistant whose entire pitch is 'I'll text you' [messaging_notifications], silent delivery failures are existential, not operational.

THE BETTER PATH: Photon's imessage-kit is open-source TypeScript infrastructure purpose-built for exactly this: AI agents that proactively reach users over iMessage [seller_pack]. 20K+ developers have stress-tested it [seller_pack]. Ditto ran 42,000+ users across five campuses with 99.87% message delivery and zero downtime during Apple throttling events [seller_pack]. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the same SDK after a $47M Series A [seller_pack]. The Advanced tier adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, voice messages, and edit/recall — the native iMessage behaviors that make an AI assistant feel like a real one, not a bot [seller_pack]. Spectrum extends the same SDK to Telegram and Discord if Lindy ever wants to go multi-channel [seller_pack].

One line, seller's voice: If Lindy's promise is 'I'll text you' — the infrastructure behind that text needs to survive Apple throttling at enterprise scale, and we've already proved it does.

THE BETTER OUTCOME: Lindy gets iMessage delivery that matches the reliability their enterprise customers expect — 99.87% delivery, zero-downtime track record during throttling events, and an open-source foundation their engineering team can audit rather than trust blindly. The proactive notification that is Lindy's core UX moment stops being a single point of failure and becomes a durable competitive moat. And if the roadmap ever calls for richer iMessage interactions — voice messages, read receipts, effects — the Advanced tier is already built [seller_pack].

generations.story:
THE GOAL: Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that replaces human assistants entirely — proactively texting users about important emails so they 'never miss what matters' [imessage_native_channel], coordinating calendars, and handling follow-ups across iMessage, SMS, Slack, and email [messaging_notifications]. The channel is not incidental: iMessage is listed as a primary chat interface in their pricing tiers [imessage_native_channel].

THE OBSTACLE: Lindy's core value prop lives or dies on message delivery. When Lindy texts a user that a critical email just landed, that message has to arrive — not eventually, not mostly. Enterprise customers from the logos on their site [enterprise_adoption] are trusting Lindy with time-sensitive decisions. Apple's iMessage infrastructure is not neutral terrain: throttling events happen, and a missed proactive notification is a broken promise to the user.

THE OLD WAY FAILS: There is no signal that Lindy has invested in a dedicated, hardened iMessage infrastructure layer — the tech stack field is empty [tech_stack]. Building and maintaining reliable iMessage delivery in-house means owning the throttling risk alone, with no community stress-testing, no open audit trail, and no published resilience proof. For an assistant whose entire pitch is 'I'll text you' [messaging_notifications], silent delivery failures are existential, not operational.

THE BETTER PATH: Photon's imessage-kit is open-source TypeScript infrastructure purpose-built for exactly this: AI agents that proactively reach users over iMessage [seller_pack]. 20K+ developers have stress-tested it [seller_pack]. Ditto ran 42,000+ users across five campuses with 99.87% message delivery and zero downtime during Apple throttling events [seller_pack]. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the same SDK after a $47M Series A [seller_pack]. The Advanced tier adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, voice messages, and edit/recall — the native iMessage behaviors that make an AI assistant feel like a real one, not a bot [seller_pack]. Spectrum extends the same SDK to Telegram and Discord if Lindy ever wants to go multi-channel [seller_pack].

One line, seller's voice: If Lindy's promise is 'I'll text you' — the infrastructure behind that text needs to survive Apple throttling at enterprise scale, and we've already proved it does.

THE BETTER OUTCOME: Lindy gets iMessage delivery that matches the reliability their enterprise customers expect — 99.87% delivery, zero-downtime track record during throttling events, and an open-source foundation their engineering team can audit rather than trust blindly. The proactive notification that is Lindy's core UX moment stops being a single point of failure and becomes a durable competitive moat. And if the roadmap ever calls for richer iMessage interactions — voice messages, read receipts, effects — the Advanced tier is already built [seller_pack].

brief.what_they_do: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that handles administrative work like email triage, meeting scheduling, and follow-ups via iMessage, SMS, and email integrations. It learns user communication style and automates inbox management, calendar coordination, and meeting prep.

brief.brief: Lindy is an AI executive assistant that automates administrative work (email, meetings, follow-ups) and communicates natively via iMessage and SMS, with enterprise adoption from Fortune 500 companies. The product is positioned as a replacement for human assistants at $49.99–$59.99/month and includes HIPAA compliance and 100+ integrations. Strong signal for agentic notifications: Lindy proactively texts users about important emails, making it a proven use case for iMessage/SMS as a primary notification and interaction channel.

event.story:
THE GOAL:
Lindy wants to be the AI executive assistant that replaces human assistants entirely — proactively texting users about important emails, scheduling meetings, and handling follow-ups so nothing falls through the cracks. Their own words: 'I'll text you about important emails so you never miss what matters.' [imessage_native_channel] [messaging_notifications] Enterprise logos like Shopify, Apple, Adobe, McKinsey, and NVIDIA signal they're already selling into high-stakes environments where reliability is non-negotiable. [enterprise_adoption]

THE OBSTACLE:
Lindy's core value prop lives or dies on the iMessage channel — it's listed as the primary chat interface across pricing tiers [imessage_native_channel], and the proactive text notification is the product's most visceral promise. But iMessage infrastructure is notoriously fragile: Apple throttles connections, accounts get flagged, and delivery silently fails. When Lindy texts a McKinsey partner about a critical email and the message doesn't arrive, the assistant has failed at the one thing it promised. There's no fallback in the brief, no resilience signal — just exposure. [messaging_notifications] [enterprise_adoption]

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Building or stitching together iMessage delivery in-house means owning the throttling problem, the account rotation problem, and the silent failure problem — all while shipping the actual product. At the enterprise tier with HIPAA compliance and Fortune 500 customers [security_compliance] [enterprise_adoption], a dropped notification isn't a UX annoyance — it's a trust event. The current stack has no confirmed iMessage infrastructure layer [tech_stack empty], which means Lindy is either running on fragile self-managed connections or a commodity SMS fallback that doesn't deliver the native iMessage experience users expect at $49.99–$59.99/month. [pricing_model]

THE BETTER PATH:
Photon's imessage-kit is open-source iMessage infrastructure built specifically for AI agents that need to reach users proactively — 20K+ developers, ~2,300 GitHub stars, github.com/photon-hq/imessage-kit [seller_pack]. Ditto runs 42,000+ users across five campuses on the SDK with 99.87% message delivery and zero downtime during Apple throttling events [seller_pack] — exactly the failure mode Lindy cannot afford with enterprise accounts. TinyFish runs enterprise web agents on the same SDK after a $47M Series A [seller_pack]. The Advanced iMessage Kit adds tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts, and voice messages — the native feel that makes Lindy feel like a real assistant, not a bot. Spectrum (photon.codes/spectrum) layers iMessage, Telegram, and Discord under one SDK for the multi-channel fallback Lindy's enterprise customers will eventually demand.

The outreach angle: your iMessage notification is the product — we built the infrastructure that keeps it alive when Apple throttles.

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy's proactive text notifications reach Fortune 500 inboxes with 99.87% delivery, survive Apple throttling events without engineering escalations, and feel native — tapbacks, typing indicators, read receipts — not SMS dressed up as iMessage. Enterprise customers on HIPAA-compliant plans get the reliability the price tag implies. And if Apple ever shifts the rules, Spectrum means Lindy reroutes to Telegram or Discord without rewriting the integration. The assistant keeps its promise.

THE OBSTACLE:
When iMessage is your core UX and Apple throttles your infrastructure, you have no fallback. Lindy's agents proactively push notifications to users via iMessage [messaging_notifications] — which means delivery reliability is not a nice-to-have, it is the product. One throttle event, one Apple policy shift, and the promise 'you'll never miss what matters' breaks. Their enterprise customers include logos like Shopify, Adobe, and McKinsey [enterprise_adoption] — organizations where a missed alert is a missed deal, not just an inconvenience. The compliance bar is also real: HIPAA, GDPR, PIPEDA, with BAA available [security_compliance]. The messaging layer touching those users has to be auditable and durable.

There is no evidence in the signals that Lindy has invested in a dedicated iMessage infrastructure layer; the feature is confirmed live [imessage_native_channel] but the underlying stack is unverified. Home-built iMessage pipes have no community stress-testing, no open audit trail, and no resilience proof against the throttling events that kill delivery silently.

THE BETTER PATH:

The angle: your agent already lives in iMessage — we built the infrastructure that keeps it there when Apple pushes back.

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lindy's 'never miss what matters' promise holds even during Apple throttling events, because the delivery layer has been proven under that exact pressure [seller_pack]. Enterprise customers on HIPAA-covered plans get an auditable, open-source channel they can inspect [seller_pack]. Typing indicators and tapbacks close the gap between 'AI notification' and 'assistant conversation,' which is the product experience Lindy is selling. And if Lindy expands to Telegram or Discord, Spectrum gives them one SDK across all three channels [seller_pack] without rebuilding the pipe.
